build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors. Returns 0 or 1; the rest of the score is multiplied by this gate.

requirements.description: The player builds enclosures on a grid by fencing areas and assigning biome types. Animals are placed in enclosures. Enclosure biome must match animal habitat requirements or the animal becomes stressed. Score 0 if there is no enclosure building, if biomes do not exist, or if habitat matching has no effect. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Visitors enter through a gate, walk paths, view enclosures, and spend money on admission and amenities. Visitor count and happiness are tracked. More visitors means more revenue. Score 0 if visitors do not appear, if they generate no revenue, or if happiness is not tracked. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

A money economy drives the zoo: admission and amenity sales generate income, animal purchases and staff salaries are expenses. A money counter is permanently visible. Score 0 if there is no money system or if everything is free. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Animal welfare is tracked per animal with a visible mood indicator. Welfare depends on enclosure size, biome match, and companion animals. Stressed animals are less attractive to visitors. Score 0 if animal welfare does not exist or if it has no gameplay effect. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

The full loop connects: title -> new zoo -> build/manage/observe -> time advances (seasons) -> zoo thrives or fails -> result screen. The game shows seasonal summaries with visitor and financial stats. Score 0 if any transition is missing or if there is no time progression. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

At least five animal species exist with different sprites, habitat requirements, popularity ratings, and costs. Score 0 if fewer than four species exist or if species are visually identical. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

At least three biome types exist (savanna, arctic, jungle, aquatic) with visually distinct enclosure appearances. Different animals require different biomes. Score 0 if biomes do not exist or if all enclosures look identical. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Visitor amenities (food stalls, gift shops, rest areas) can be built and affect visitor happiness and spending. At least three amenity types exist. Score 0 if no amenities exist or if they have no effect. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Breeding is possible when compatible animals share a well-maintained enclosure. Baby animals increase visitor draw and can be kept or traded. Score 0 if breeding does not exist or if it has no gameplay effect. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Conservation goals provide bonus objectives (breed endangered species, maintain genetic diversity, reach welfare targets). Completing goals earns rewards or unlocks. Score 0 if no goals exist beyond profit. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

The HUD shows money, visitor count, overall animal welfare, and current season in clear non-overlapping regions. Score 0 if money, visitors, or welfare is missing. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The zoo view clearly shows paths, enclosures with distinct biomes, animals inside enclosures, visitors walking, and amenities. The build menu and play area are separated. Score 0 if enclosures are indistinguishable or if animals are invisible. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Actions produce visible feedback: building shows structures appear, placing animals shows them in enclosures, visitors react to exhibits, welfare changes show mood indicators, and breeding shows baby animals. Score 0 if actions produce no observable change. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Overall art coherence: enclosures, animals, paths, visitors, amenities, and UI belong to a consistent bright, educational zoo visual language with lush natural tones. Score 0 if the screen mixes incompatible styles or reads as plain default Godot grey. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Animal sprites are real illustrated or pixel-art creatures with distinct species appearances. Visitors are character sprites, not dots. Score 0 if animals are plain colored shapes or if visitors are featureless. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Enclosures use real textured biome tiles (grass, snow, jungle foliage, water) with environmental detail. Paths and amenities have illustrated appearances. Score 0 if enclosures are flat colored areas. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

HUD and UI use styled themed widgets with nature-park chrome: animal cards have illustrated frames, welfare indicators have themed icons, and menus have natural-material backgrounds. Score 0 if UI elements are unstyled Godot defaults. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Animation and atmosphere: animals move and idle within enclosures, visitors walk and stop at exhibits, amenities show activity, and the zoo has ambient life (birds, rustling leaves, water features). Score 0 if all elements are static. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.
